stories.title: Keir Starmer resigns — Andy Burnham set to become UK Prime Minister
stories.summary: Andy Burnham has confirmed he will run to succeed Keir Starmer as Labour leader and UK Prime Minister after Starmer's resignation. Burnham is widely expected to take office this month.

stories.title: Starmer: 'It was really tough to accept my political career was over'
stories.summary: In a candid BBC interview, outgoing PM Keir Starmer said he had 'saved' the Labour Party and promised to 'keep my mouth shut' under his successor. He warned Burnham will face the same demands of foreign affairs as any premier.

stories.title: Downing Street extends pub hours for England's World Cup campaign
stories.summary: The government extended pub opening hours in England after the nation progressed in the World Cup, a decision Downing Street said had to be made quickly once England's qualification was confirmed.

stories.title: Commons schedule: 6–10 July debates and e-petitions
stories.summary: The UK Parliament has published its forthcoming Commons schedule, including Westminster Hall debates on e-petitions selected by the Petitions Committee.

stories.title: Commons debate on extended producer responsibility for glass recycling
stories.summary: MPs debated the extended producer responsibility scheme, which is seeing breweries, pubs and other businesses pay twice to recycle glass bottles, raising concerns about its impact on the hospitality sector.

topics.name: Labour Party
topics.description: Labour Party news, policy & internal politics

stories.title: Labour general secretary Hollie Ridley to stand down after party conference
stories.summary: Hollie Ridley, credited with organising the ground campaign behind Labour's 2024 landslide, has announced she will step down as general secretary after the party conference. She said she was making way for a successor to work alongside the new leader.

stories.title: Burnham rules out calling an early general election as PM
stories.summary: Andy Burnham, the newly elected MP for Makerfield and expected next PM, has ruled out calling an early general election and confirmed he will stick to the 2024 Labour manifesto. He also indicated backing for electoral reform.

stories.title: Labour MPs seek cap on political donations
stories.summary: A group of Labour MPs are pushing for a cap on political donations, believing the measure will be backed by dozens of colleagues and likely next PM Andy Burnham.

stories.title: Badenoch attacks Burnham for 'shrinking from scrutiny'
stories.summary: Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch launched a pointed attack on Andy Burnham, accusing the Labour frontrunner of avoiding traditional media questions and dodging scrutiny as he closes in on the premiership.

stories.title: Badenoch on the campaign trail in Hertfordshire
stories.summary: The Conservative leader visited Hertfordshire as part of her ongoing effort to rebuild the party, meeting local supporters ahead of the Manchester mayor by-election.

stories.title: Lib Dems score three council by-election wins against Conservatives
stories.summary: The Liberal Democrats gained seats from the Conservatives in Powys and held strong in the Cotswolds in recent local council by-elections, extending their run of success at Conservative expense.

stories.title: Reform UK-led council fails to attract any sponsors for union flag scheme
stories.summary: A Reform UK-led local council's initiative to fly union flags — backed by Lee Anderson and Robert Jenrick — has failed to attract any sponsors, raising questions about the party's grassroots credibility in local government.

stories.title: Manchester mayor by-election a key test for Reform UK
stories.summary: The 'biggest ever by-election' — for Manchester mayor — will test whether Reform UK can convert national polling support into regional electoral wins, as Burnham's allies prepare for the race.

stories.title: New Scottish Parliament Presiding Officer Kenneth Gibson meets King Charles
stories.summary: Cunninghame North MSP Kenneth Gibson was formally received by King Charles III in his new role as Presiding Officer of the Scottish Parliament following recent Holyrood elections.

stories.title: Kate Forbes: 'I was slam dunk for SNP leader until gay marriage views'
stories.summary: Kate Forbes has claimed she was seen as the obvious frontrunner to lead the SNP until she publicly stated her conservative Christian views on gay marriage, which she says cost her the leadership.

stories.title: Scrutiny is more important than ever in the new Holyrood session
stories.summary: With the SNP remaining the largest party but significant changes in composition, analysis suggests the new Scottish Parliament session demands stronger scrutiny of government as Reform UK establishes a presence in Holyrood.

stories.title: Sir Charlie Mayfield appointed Co-Chair of Keep Britain Working Vanguard Phase
stories.summary: The Government has appointed Sir Charlie Mayfield to co-chair the Keep Britain Working Vanguard Phase alongside Secretaries of State for Work and Pensions, as part of a cross-department push to improve employment outcomes.

stories.title: 'Biggest ever by-election' to test Burnham's credentials and nation's mood
stories.summary: The Manchester mayor by-election is being described as the largest by-election in British history and will be a major test of Andy Burnham's ability to turn the tide against Reform UK as he prepares to take office as PM.

stories.title: Lib Dems gain from Conservatives in Powys council by-elections
stories.summary: The Liberal Democrats won three council by-election seats, continuing their strong record of gains from the Conservatives in local contests.

stories.title: House of Lords debates Nature's Rights Bill
stories.summary: The House of Lords held a debate on 3 July on the Nature's Rights Bill (HL), with Green peer Natalie Bennett arguing that nature's rights are an essential foundation for a healthy, secure future.

stories.title: Lords debate live music industry support
stories.summary: On 2 July, the House of Lords held a question for short debate on the live music sector, raising concerns about the pressures facing venues and artists across the UK.

stories.title: Lords Chamber sitting on 3 July — ODA cuts and welfare debates
stories.summary: The Lords Chamber was sitting on 3 July, with peers expected to question the government on official development assistance cuts and broader welfare pressures affecting women and girls globally.
